## LEVEL 1 — BLOCKING (sin esto, no hay submission)

- [ ] **1.1 Decision: ptealc/gamma NDVI removal (Option A vs B)** | Guillermo | Decision record
  Instruction: Review results/diagnostics/coefficient_stability.csv. The pr coefficient in ptealc/gamma changes sign from +0.162 to -0.717 when NDVI is removed. Choose Option B (remove NDVI, AIC cost +8, pr becomes interpretable) or Option A (retain, document limitation). Record decision in master_analysis_report.md.
  Unblocks: scripts/10, all attribution figures

- [ ] **1.2 Fix scaling parameter mismatch in attribution** | Claude Code | Corrected script
  Instruction: In scripts/8_counterfactual_attribution.R, the load_species_data() function recomputes scaling via scale() on filtered rows. This differs from scripts/4 which scales on ALL rows before filtering. Fix by either:
  (a) Saving train_dyn_scale from scripts/4 and loading it in scripts/8, or
  (b) Applying the same row filter in scripts/4 before scaling (would require refitting models)
  Option (a) is the correct fix. Add to scripts/4 after line 252:
  ```r
  saveRDS(train_dyn_scale, here("results", paste0(sp, "_train_dyn_scale.rds")))
  ```
  Then in scripts/8 load_species_data(), replace the scale() block with:
  ```r
  train_scale <- readRDS(here("results", paste0(sp, "_train_dyn_scale.rds")))
  ```
  Unblocks: Attribution correctness for all species

- [ ] **1.3 Refit pteori/epsilon without NDVI** | Raul or Claude Code | results/pteori_epsilon_revised.rds
  Instruction: After decision 1.1, if pteori/epsilon NDVI is to be removed:
  ```r
  # RAUL: can run locally (< 5 min)
  cfg_revised <- get_model_config("pteori")
  cfg_revised$epsilon_formula <- ~ Land_Cover_Type_1_Percent_Class_12 + pr
  cfg_revised$epsilon_vars <- c("Land_Cover_Type_1_Percent_Class_12", "pr")
  mod_revised <- colext(
    psiformula = cfg_revised$psi_formula,
    gammaformula = cfg_revised$gamma_formula,
    epsilonformula = cfg_revised$epsilon_formula,
    pformula = cfg_revised$p_formula,
    data = occ_umf
  )
  saveRDS(mod_revised, "results/pteori_epsilon_revised.rds")
  # Verify: LC12 coefficient should be more negative than -0.09 (the value without NDVI)
  # If LC12 recovers to ~ -0.50 or more negative, the revision is successful
  ```
  Unblocks: Attribution Tasks 3-5

  # RAUL: was n.batch=800, batch.length=25 (20,000 total)

  n.batch      <- 4000        # 4000 x 25 = 100,000 total
  batch.length <- 25

- [ ] **1.6 parboot GoF (fix formlist bug + nsim=500)** | Raul (cluster) | results/{sp}_gof_parboot.rds
  Instruction: The current parboot fails with "no slot named formlist" error. This is a serialization issue — models lose the formlist slot when saved/loaded via RDS.
  Fix: Run parboot in the SAME R session where the model is fitted (do not load from RDS). Alternatively, update unmarked to latest version and test.
  ```r
  # RAUL: In scripts/4_occupancy_models.R
  # Line 63: NSIM_PARBOOT <- 500   # was 10
  # Line 64: NSIM_MB_GOF  <- 500   # was 10
  # Run parboot IMMEDIATELY after fitting, before saveRDS()
  # Test with nsim=5 first to confirm formlist error is resolved
  # Estimated runtime: ~50x current per species
  ```
  Unblocks: Methods 2.4, c-hat reporting, Table 7

- [ ] **1.7 Increase blockCV block size to >= 264 km** | Claude Code or Raul | Re-run scripts/5
  Instruction: In scripts/5_validation.R, line 158:
  ```r
  # Was: size = 50000  (50 km)
  # Change to:
  size = 270000  # 270 km (> 264 km, the largest spatial range from P. alchata)
  ```
  This will produce fewer spatial blocks (potentially 2-3 instead of ~20), making cross-validation less stable but spatially honest. If only 2 blocks result, consider:
  - Using leave-one-block-out instead of 5-fold
  - Setting size to the median spatial range (~107 km) and documenting the limitation
  Unblocks: Validation integrity for reviewer confidence

## LEVEL 2 — STRONGLY RECOMMENDED (sin esto, la revision sera dura)

- [ ] **2.1 Naive vs corrected gamma comparison** | Claude Code | New analysis block
  Instruction: Add to scripts/8_effort_confounding.R or new script:
  ```r
  # For each species, compare:
  # naive_gamma: proportion of sites transitioning 0->1 in raw detection history
  # corrected_gamma: model-estimated colonisation from colext
  for (sp in species_codes) {
    umf <- readRDS(paste0("results/results2/", sp, "_v4b_umf.rds"))
    y <- umf@y
    T <- umf@numPrimary; J <- ncol(y) / T
    y_3d <- array(y, dim = c(nrow(y), J, T))
    occ_t <- apply(y_3d[,,1:(T-1)] > 0, c(1,3), max, na.rm = TRUE)
    occ_t1 <- apply(y_3d[,,2:T] > 0, c(1,3), max, na.rm = TRUE)
    # Sites unoccupied in t that become occupied in t+1
    naive_col <- (occ_t == 0 & occ_t1 == 1)
    naive_gamma <- mean(naive_col / (occ_t == 0), na.rm = TRUE)
    mod <- readRDS(paste0("results/", sp, "_model_object.rds"))
    corrected_gamma <- mean(predict(mod, type = "col")$Predicted, na.rm = TRUE)
    cat(sp, ": naive=", naive_gamma, " corrected=", corrected_gamma, "\n")
  }
  ```
  Unblocks: Discussion 4.4

## LEVEL 3 — OPTIONAL BUT VALUABLE

- [ ] **3.1 Recovery threshold analysis** | Claude Code | Discussion paragraph
  Instruction: Using psi* = gamma/(gamma+epsilon), solve for the gamma increase needed to achieve psi* = 10%: gamma_needed = 0.10 * epsilon / 0.90. Compare with current gamma to quantify the gap.

- [ ] **3.2 Informative phi priors for stPGOcc** | Raul | Modified script
  Instruction: Based on species ecology:
  ```r
  # T. tetrax/O. tarda: expect ~50 km range -> phi ~ 0.06
  # P. alchata: expect ~300 km range -> phi ~ 0.01
  priors.list$phi.unif <- c(3/500, 3/10)  # range 10-500 km
  ```

  Instruction: With only 18 extinction events and SE > 30, ptealc epsilon with 2 covariates is overparameterised. Consider epsilon ~ 1. AIC comparison will determine if this is justified.
